programmes.show_description: The annual bowls club outing seems to provide an ideal opportunity for Cecil to be alone with May. S2 E7: A Bowl In The Hand
Ron agrees to help Gary return to 1940 but insists he go with him, and Phoebe loses half her belongings in a bombing. S1 E4: The More I See You
Mr. Bean gets to an appointment with the dentist after oversleeping and has trouble in the park with a bothersome fly. E5: The Trouble with Mr. Bean
Drama series about a detective who wakes up in what seems to be 1973. Sam and Annie are led into a world of suburban swingers' parties to solve a murder case. Starring John Simm. S2 E4
When a new suave man moves in downstairs owning a sportscar, Sandra and Beryl begin to make their move on him - however, following Llandudno might be a surprise too far. S2 E7: The New Neighbour
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Brenda gets soaking wet while she and Malcolm are returning from bird watching; as Madge has to wash all Brenda's clothes, everyone else is talking about redundancy or job changes. S2 E1: Falling
When Darryl dies, Tracey has to decide what kind of a send-off to give him - Sharon wants it to be nice for the boys and Dorien has got the perfect outfit. S12 E1: Knocking on Heaven's Door
When the hapless bumbler Mr. Bean goes back to school, he scares a boy in the chemistry lab and the nudes in the art class cause him to look at the floor. E11: Back to School, Mr. Bean
Returning from a boat trip to Tangier, Amy is approached by a Spaniard who asks her to deliver a box allegedly containing the ashes of his dead friend Pepe to his widowed mother. S2 E5: Pepe

Hit '70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, who are forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S5, E1: Make Yourself at Home
David is less than happy to find that the obnoxious Kev Wilson, a man who has always done better than him and let him know it since childhood, is staying at the hotel. S2 E6: Snap
Alf tells Sir Ralph that Lord Meldrum can't be having an affair with Lady Agatha because he was wounded in the Royal Artillery. S2 E5 The Wounds of War

Miss Jones is collecting for a famine charity when Philip challenges Rigsby to go 48 hours without eating - Rigsby gladly accepts the offer but soon finds his will weakening. S2 E2: Food Glorious Food
When Wolfie, Ken, Tucker and Speed find an abandoned tank, Wolfie decides that they should drive it to the Houses of Parliament. S3 E7: The Glorious Day
Questions trouble the Boswell household - will Ma be reunited with Freddie, and what really happened to Uncle Charlie's money? S4 E3
Sandra's friend, a posh beautiful actress, comes to stay but soon puts Beryl's nose out of joint when she takes an interest with both of their dates for a night. S2 E6: Three's a Crowd
The station is prepared for a visit by the chairman of British Railways, but it turns out to be a different Doctor Beeching altogether. S2 E8: Action Stations
Alf tells Sir Ralph that Lord Meldrum can't be having an affair with Lady Agatha because he was wounded in the Royal Artillery. S2 E5 The Wounds of War
The youngsters want a party, but George - recalling the last one - is against the idea. Mildred, however, is all for it and gives it her blessing... S6 E1: The Party's Over
Ranjeet plans to kill himself because of a girl he has to marry. The girl herself appears at the school looking for him, which prompts frantic plans to be made. S1 E3: Fate Worse Than Death
Alf complains to the DHSS that he should have been paid more winter's heating allowance. He has a loud revealing dream in front of his friends. S3 E6

Arthur needs to raise some cash fast, but will insurance fraud and pawning the jewellery of 'er indoors' raise more problems than it will solve? S10 E1: A Fridge Too Far

Gary believes that he married Phoebe after learning of an elderly woman name Phoebe Sparrow. S1 E5: I Get Along Without You Very Well
Rowan Atkinson creates more comic mayhem as Mr. Bean comes to the aid of a heart attack victim, fails miserably to post a letter, and flies off on holiday. E6: Mr. Bean Rides Again
Drama series about a detective who wakes up in what seems to be 1973. When a man's wife and daughter are kidnapped and held hostage, Sam tries to focus on the investigation. Starring John Simm. S2 E5
The girls are arguing - they need more space and decide to move to a bigger flat, but they need a third person to share the high rent. Can they find anybody? S2 E8: The New Flat

Pamela gets even more depressed at work as Terry gets roped into an effort to get her back out into circulation, and Brenda isn't happy when she spots Malcolm talking to another woman. S2 E2: Dancing
Tracey's house has been burgled during Darryl's funeral, and the family decide that it's up to them to track down their possessions. S12 E2: Too Much of Nothing
It turns our that Mr. Bean isn't very good at looking after things in a laundrette, and he later has difficulty keeping control of his ball during a round of mini golf. E12: Tee Off, Mr. Bean

'70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, who are forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S5, E2: Compliments of the Season
The last night of the holiday finds both couples packing, but David and Linda decide to slip away and meet for one last time. S2 E7: Adios
The Honourable Teddy has a bad dream and a mysterious stranger arrives at night in need of help - the servants oblige as best they can. S2 E6 Stranger in the Night
Health and fitness are the new buzzwords in the house when Rigsby's keenness to portray himself in prime condition leads him to challenge Philip to a boxing match. S2 E3: A Body Like Mine
The Tooting Popular Front are stuck in prison after stealing a tank until impresario Benny Salzberg arrives with a plan. S4 E1: Bigger Than Guy Fawkes
Jack gets a clean bill of health, Billy's marriage is in trouble already and Ma's relationship with Freddie reaches another crossroads. S4 E4
When a new suave man moves in downstairs owning a sportscar, Sandra and Beryl begin to make their move on him - however, following Llandudno might be a surprise too far. S2 E7: The New Neighbour
It's Jack and May's wedding anniversary, but May is worried that she might be pregnant - meanwhile, Arnold fears that he is about to get the sack. S2 E9: A Pregnant Pause

When the lease is up on the flat and George wants to increase the rent, the flatmates decide that the only way to raise the money is to get a fourth tenant to share. S6 E2: One More for the Pot
The students' English is not improving and Mr. Brown decides to detain them after class. The long evening is made worse when they get locked inside the classroom. S1 E4: All Through the Night
Christmas is a time to be with the family, but not for Alf Garnett. He is destined to spend Christmas in hospital, having his hip replaced. In Sickness and In Health Christmas Special 1987

When Arthur, Dave and Ray take a trip across the English Channel to take advantage of duty-free prices, it's Arthur who ultimately gets taken advantage of. S10 E2: Another Case of Van Blank
